mission.title: vllm: Support Python 3.13
mission.description: Add Python 3.13 support to vLLM. Python 3.13 was released on October 7, 2024, and most ML-related packages have added support. Users had to downgrade Python just for vLLM. Requested by 27+ users.

steps.title: Check your Python version
steps.description:
Verify your Python and vLLM versions:
```bash
python --version
pip show vllm | grep Version
```
Python 3.13 support requires an updated version of vLLM and its dependencies.

steps.title: Set up a Python 3.13 environment
steps.description:
Create a virtual environment with Python 3.13:
```bash
# Using uv (recommended)
uv venv --python 3.13 ~/venvs/py313
source ~/venvs/py313/bin/activate

# Or using standard venv
python3.13 -m venv ~/venvs/py313
source ~/venvs/py313/bin/activate
```

steps.title: Install vLLM on Python 3.13
steps.description:
Install vLLM in the Python 3.13 environment:
```bash
# Using precompiled wheels (faster)
VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1 pip install vllm

# Or using uv with CUDA 12.8 backend
VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1 uv pip install -U vllm --torch-backend=cu128
```
The precompiled option avoids building from source, which can be slow.

steps.title: Verify vLLM works on Python 3.13
steps.description:
Test that vLLM runs correctly:
```bash
python -c "import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)"

# Start a test server
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B
```
You should see the server start successfully without any Python 3.13 compatibility errors.

resolution.summary: vLLM added Python 3.13 support by updating dependencies and build configurations to be compatible with Python 3.13. Users can now install and run vLLM on Python 3.13 without needing to downgrade their Python version.
codeSnippets:
# Set up Python 3.13 environment and install vLLM
uv venv --python 3.13 ~/venvs/py313
source ~/venvs/py313/bin/activate
VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1 uv pip install -U vllm --torch-backend=cu128

# Verify it works
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B

prerequisites.description: Python 3.13+, pip (or uv), a CUDA-capable GPU, and CUDA toolkit 12.x.
